explanations.6: os.path.splitext() splits a filename into two parts: the name and the extension; [1] grabs just the extension part like '.py'.
explanations.22: dirs[:] = [...] modifies the list in-place so os.walk() won't descend into those folders; this filters out hidden folders and Python cache folders.
explanations.26: setdefault() is a shortcut that returns the category's set if it exists, or creates an empty set and returns it; then add() puts the filename in that set.
explanations.36: sorted(list(files)) converts the set to a sorted list because sets have no guaranteed order, and we want consistent file ordering.
explanations.41: This removes parentheses and square brackets from filenames because Mermaid (the diagram tool) uses those characters for its own syntax and gets confused otherwise.
explanations.61: subprocess.run() executes the 'mmdc' command-line tool (a Mermaid diagram compiler) with various options; check=True makes it raise an error if the command fails.
